analysis.summary: The updated skill produces 100% pass rate on all examples/tests assertions vs 0% for the old skill. This is expected — the old skill had no instructions for creating test stubs or examples at all.
observations: All 20 assertions are perfectly discriminating — they pass 100% with the new skill and 0% with the old, confirming the feature gap was real.
Token usage is ~20% higher with the updated skill (49K vs 41K mean), reflecting the additional work of reading reference examples/tests and generating more files.
Duration is ~35% longer (229s vs 170s mean) for the same reason — more files to plan and describe.
The Go with-skill run produced 117 files total (39 source, 63 tests, 8 examples) vs the old skill's ~20 files (14 source stubs + config).
Java run demonstrated correct language-specific conventions: PascalCase test names (ExecutorTest.java), JUnit 5 @Test + fail(), camelCase methods.
Rust MCP run correctly identified language-specific gaps: #[cfg(test)] convention not mentioned, mod.rs structure for nested test directories.
potential_improvements: Test naming convention is hardcoded as test_{module} (Python style) in the template — should note language-specific variants (FooTest.java, foo_test.go, foo.test.ts)
Examples tree should signal it's not exhaustive (add ... or note about reference sync catching additional files)
Step 2 summary size target should be bumped from ~5-8KB to ~6-10KB to accommodate EXAMPLES and TESTS sections
